items.claim: Paddy Pimblett lacks the power in his hands to significantly hurt Benoit Saint Denis.
items.quote: I don't think Patty has a ton of power in his hands.

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis is stiff on the feet.
items.quote: I know that BSD has power, but he's stiff on the feet, man.

items.claim: Both Paddy Pimblett and Benoit Saint Denis have poor striking defense.
items.quote: Both guys have bad striking defense.

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett has a better submission game and is a better jiu-jitsu player than Benoit Saint Denis.
items.quote: I think Patty has a better submission game and is also a better jiu-jitsu player than BSD.

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis is a better wrestler than Paddy Pimblett.
items.quote: I think his wrestling is better than Patty

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis has improved his tactical approach since training with Nicholas.
items.quote: I think he's well coached with with Nicholas. They come in with a much more tactical approach.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is coming off a five-year layoff and a catastrophic leg injury.
items.quote: Conor McGregor back after five years away coming off of a catastrophic leg injury

items.claim: Max Holloway's footwork and defensive elusiveness have improved significantly since his first fight with McGregor.
items.quote: Max Holloway's footwork is much better than what it was the first time that these guys fought.

items.claim: Conor McGregor hits harder now than he did in his first fight against Max Holloway.
items.quote: I think McGregor doesn't move necessarily the way he does, you know, he did back then, but I think he hits harder now than he does then or did then.

items.claim: Fighting at 170 lbs favors Conor McGregor.
items.quote: And I believe that to a degree that favors Connor.
